experiment: T1 Glass-Ceiling Push — Slot D (anatomical mixture-of-experts × per-item routing)

fwer_family_members.skip_axis: stacking-blender collapse at N=93 / isomorphism to iter34

codex_run1_55804.key_quote: A is the only defensible Slot D probe, but my honest prior is P(strict)<0.20 for all three. Most likely final decision: do not promote to LOOCV.

gemini_run1_55804.key_quote: The 'wall' at 0.7366 is not an architectural limitation; it is the Shannon limit of 1751 handcrafted features on 93 subjects. Preserve your FWER budget for deterministic signal extraction (Slot C) or fundamentally new data.

codex_run2_60847.key_quote: All three are below P(strict gate)<0.20, and B/C are direct iter34 reparameterizations. A has the only plausible new inductive bias, but it is still same-manifold gated ensembling and likely fails under the N≈93 wall.

gemini_run2_60847.load_bearing_critique: Violates 'NO composite blends (F53/F56/F58)'. The fold-local Ridge gate on top of partitioned experts is a classic stacking blender that will reliably collapse under N=92 LOOCV constraints.
gemini_run2_60847.key_quote: Initiating Slot D will consume compute and FWER budget for a guaranteed negative result.

kimi_run1_55804.load_bearing_critique: Stacking blender on correlated V2 experts. Violates F53/F56 composite-blend ban implicitly.
kimi_run1_55804.key_quote: 12th wall data point on the same-manifold stacking axis — same lesson as F53/F56/F58, slightly different parameterization.

kimi_run2_60847.note: Run did not finalize a clean RANK ORDER block under tri_consult time budget; treat as duplicate of run-1; convergence is 6-of-6 if counting all completed responses or 5-of-5 if counting only RC=0.

load_bearing_skip_critiques: Candidate A's per-item Ridge gate over 4 expert outputs IS a stacked-meta blender at N=93
Candidate B (per-item adaptive K) is iter34 + inner-CV-tuned K — isomorphic (codex-run2, gemini-run2)
Candidate C (tree-distance item-similarity) is learned-DAG RegressorChain — isomorphic to F70 (codex-run1, codex-run2, gemini-run1, gemini-run2)
Honest prior P(strict gate 0.99) ≤ 0.10-0.15 for the best candidate

wall_classification: Composite-blend collapse at N=93 — slot D candidate A's per-item Ridge gate is structurally a 4-element stacked-meta blender, joining F53/F56/F58 wall data points on blend mechanisms. Per-item adaptive K and tree-distance item-similarity variants reduce to iter34 and F70 reparameterizations respectively. No 4th orthogonal candidate identified that does not change loss family (slot A failed) or introduce per-subject latents (slot B failed) or violate F53/F56/F58.
slow_think_falsifier_proposed: If executed (override), item 13 (posture, axial-only) should specifically lift if anatomical MoE mechanism is real. Tri-CLI predicted instead: gate weight instability across 93 folds will spike per-item LOOCV variance; item-level deltas will be noise. Falsifier-of-falsifier: gemini-run1 expected outcome explicitly states 'Item 13 CCC shows a verifiable isolated lift (+0.02 to +0.04) ... However, the T1 total CCC lands between 0.7320 and 0.7380 ... fails the strict paired-bootstrap >0.99 gate.' I.e. mechanism partially real, but variance compounding cancels the gain.
honest_paper_claim_unchanged: T1 LOOCV CCC = 0.7366 (iter34 hybrid F70) remains the strongest WearGait-PD T1 candidate. Ceiling-push session 2026-05-08 added two new wall data points: F35-A (ordinal cum-link loss family does not improve over MSE on summed-residual targets at this N) and F35-D (anatomical mixture-of-experts × per-item Ridge gating collapses to a stacked-meta blender at N=93 under the F53/F56/F58 wall; per-item adaptive K and item-similarity-graph variants are isomorphic to iter34 / F70). Total compute spent on slot D pre-execution diligence: ~12 min of tri-CLI consult.
